summary: The team is in full execution mode for the September launch of the joint CKGSB-IE ‘New Chinese Playbook’ report on Chinese companies going global to Southeast Asia, while simultaneously launching the Asia Start campaign and building the new Intelligent China program.
summaryDetail: The top priority is the ‘New Chinese Playbook’ report, with a one-pager proposal due from Felix, bulk translation underway, and op-ed contributors and alumni case studies being sourced by Julie. The report is set to launch at events in Singapore in mid-September, with Felix confirming the Tech in Asia or Tyson event. Simultaneously, the Asia Start campaign has just launched with two routes (AI vs traditional), and the Intelligent China program website is being built by Joseph, with SCM campaign planning starting. This week’s urgent tasks include finalizing Prof Zhu Yang’s article, translating Prof Li Wei’s FT Chinese piece, and sending the Summer Davos press release to Prof Sun. Upcoming: a Monday 3pm meeting with Flow for brochure review, and continued work on the report and program launches.

meetings.title: New Chinese Playbook Report Planning
meetings.summary: Team discusses joint report with CKGSB on Chinese companies globalizing to Southeast Asia, planning content, events, and distribution for September launch.

meetings.title: Asia Start & New Program Planning
meetings.summary: Discussed Asia Start campaign with two routes (AI vs traditional), reviewed results, and introduced a new 'Intelligent China' program targeting foreigners with modules in Beijing, Shanghai, and Shenzhen.

meetings.title: EU-China Cooperation Discussion
meetings.summary: Discussion on EU-China relations, focusing on geopolitical tensions, de-risking, and the role of companies versus governments in market operations.

actionItems.text: Draft one-pager proposal for report structure and promotion plan
actionItems.detail: Create a one-pager summarizing the report structure, title, and promotion plan to show to the president.

actionItems.text: Translate bulk of Chinese report into English
actionItems.detail: Translate the core research from Professor Leeway's Chinese report for the English version.
Find CKGSB contributors like President Santiago or Professor Ma Ping for op-eds.

actionItems.detail: Meet with Tech in Asia tomorrow to see if the event theme aligns and if report can be launched there.

actionItems.detail: Meet with Tyson tomorrow to discuss using their event for report distribution.

actionItems.text: Launch Asia Start campaign with two routes for first month

actionItems.detail: Joseph to work with Daisy and Contran to finalize the webpage.

actionItems.text: Plan SCM campaign for Intelligent China program
actionItems.detail: Decide on campaign approach (AI or traditional) after webpage is ready.

actionItems.meetingTitle: Re: Translation: Ouyang Hui — The Resilience Code
actionItems.detail: Ira will review the translation and revert to Joseph.

decisions.text: Report will be jointly published by CKGSB and IE Business School.
Working title: 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global'.
Report will include a main research feature, op-eds from both schools, and industry case studies.
Target launch at events in Singapore in mid-September (Tech in Asia or Tyson).
Report will be digital first, with promotion through end of year.
Asia Start will run two parallel routes for the first month: AI-driven and traditional.
Intelligent China program will target foreigners with English-language modules, starting with module one in Beijing.
Joseph will translate Prof Li Wei's FT Chinese article.
decisions.meetingTitle: Re: 长江商学院媒体监测日报2026-6-25

decisions.text: Julie will finalize Prof Zhu Yang's article against the Chinese draft.

decisions.text: Ira will send the press release to Prof Sun on WeChat.

decisions.text: Kaylee and Xuan will take over publishing academic seminars on the website.

decisions.text: All final files will be saved to the school's Cloud.
Meeting scheduled for Monday 3pm with Flow for brochure review.
